返回本批次

黑客入侵 A16Z 支持的电话农场，称其为“反基督者”

Hacker compromises A16Z-backed phone farm, calling them the 'antichrist'

www.404media.co 作者 wibbily 分数 102 评论 27 2026/04/14 11:32
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 根据 404 Media 看到的屏幕截图，一名黑客入侵了 Doublespeed 的后端系统。Doublespeed 是一家由 a16z 资助的初创公司，该公司利用手机农场，用人工智能生成的 TikTok 账户充斥社交媒体，并试图让这些账户发布称 a16z 为“反基督者”的表情包。这次黑客攻击至少是 Doublespeed 第二次遭到入侵。该初创公司使用人工智能来创建假影响者、生成视频和发布评论。“a16z 是反基督者。由 doublespeed.ai 赞助，”该模因说道。其中包括 a16z 联合创始人 Marc Andreessen 的照片；一名女子钢管舞；以及神秘符号 Baphomet。💡您还知道有关此突破口或 Doublespeed 的其他信息吗？我们很乐意听取您的意见。使用非工作设备，您可以在 Signal（joseph.404）上安全地向 Joseph 发送消息，或者在 emanuel.404（emanuel.404）上安全地向 Joseph 发送消息。 屏幕截图显示，该模因在 Doublespeed 客户的仪表板上排队等待发布，似乎是要发布到其关联的社交媒体帐户。标题表明黑客窃取了一些其他数据，并可能试图发布数百个帐户的内容。标题写道：“47MB 被泄露。573 个帐户可发布。413 部手机被丢弃。A16z 产品组合安全性构建不同。”模因的屏幕截图。图片：404 Media。看来该表情包最终并未发布在 Doublespeed 客户的社交媒体帐户上。一张屏幕截图包含受影响的 Doublespeed 帐户的社交媒体账号；截至周一，该表情包在该帐户上不可用。Doublespeed 联合创始人 Zuhair Lakhani 在一封电子邮件中告诉 404 Media，“我们意识到未经授权的访问尝试，并迅速解决了这个问题。这涉及到一个旧的帖子排队系统，为了与现有客户工作流程兼容，我们一直保留该系统，此后我们已经保护了它。”“重要的是，没有成功发布未经授权的帖子，我们也没有看到任何证据表明这种尝试对客户产生了更广泛的影响，”他补充道。
  2. 404 Media 去年首次报道了 Doublespeed，当时该初创公司从 a16z 筹集了 100 万美元，作为其“Speedrun”加速器计划的一部分，“Speedrun”是“一个快节奏的、为期 12 周的启动计划，指导创始人度过成长的每个关键阶段。” Doublespeed 推销其使用手机农场的方式，以此规避社交媒体平台禁止消除不真实行为的政策。 Doublespeed 客户可以访问仪表板，使他们能够操作多个人工智能生成的影响者。目前Doublespeed专注于运营TikTok账户，但也计划让客户能够在X和Instagram上运营账户。 Doublespeed 此前曾于 2025 年 12 月遭到黑客攻击。那次黑客攻击的数据显示，Doublespeed 运营着至少 400 个 TikTok 帐户，其中至少 200 个帐户在 TikTok 上积极推广产品，其中大多数没有透露它们是广告或不是真人。这些人工智能生成的帐户推广的一些产品包括补充剂、按摩器和约会应用程序。正如我们去年所指出的，马克·安德森 (Marc Andreessen) 也是 Meta 的董事会成员，安德森·霍洛维茨 (Andreessen Horowitz) 一半的股份就是以他的名字命名的。 Meta 没有回答我们关于其一名董事会成员支持一家公然违反其“真实身份表示”政策的公司的问题。
  3. 关于作者 约瑟夫是一位屡获殊荣的调查记者，专注于产生影响力。他的行为引发了价值数亿美元的罚款、关闭科技公司等等。关于作者 Emanuel Maiberg 对那些鲜为人知的社区和塑造技术、麻烦制造者和小问题的流程感兴趣。给他发电子邮件 emanuel@404media.co

Original Summary

  1. A hacker has compromised a backend system for Doublespeed, an a16z-funded startup that uses a phone farm to flood social media with AI-generated TikTok accounts, and attempted to have those accounts post memes calling a16z the “antichrist,” according to screenshots seen by 404 Media.The hack is at least the second time Doublespeed has been compromised. The startup uses AI to create fake influencers, generate videos, and post comments.“a16z is the antichrist. sponsored by doublespeed.ai,” the meme says. It includes images of a16z co-founder Marc Andreessen; a woman pole dancing; and occult symbol Baphomet.💡Do you know anything else about this breach or Doublespeed? We would love to hear from you. Using a non-work device, you can message Joseph securely on Signal at joseph.404 or Emanuel on emanuel.404.The screenshots show the meme queued up for publication in Doublespeed customers’ dashboard, seemingly to post to their associated social media accounts. A caption indicates the hacker stole some other data and may tried to post content from hundreds of accounts.“47MB exfiltrated. 573 accounts postable. 413 phones dumped. A16z portfolio security built different,” the caption reads. A screenshot of the meme. Image: 404 Media.It appears the meme was ultimately not posted on Doublespeed customers’ social media accounts. One screenshot included the social media handle of an impacted Doublespeed account; as of Monday, the meme was not available on that account.Zuhair Lakhani, a co-founder of Doublespeed, told 404 Media in an email “We’re aware of the unauthorized access attempt and addressed it quickly. This involved an older system for queuing posts that had remained in place for compatibility with existing customer workflows, and we have since secured it.”“Importantly, no unauthorized posts were successfully published, and we have not seen evidence that this attempt resulted in broader impact to customers,” he added.
  2. 404 Media first reported about Doublespeed last year, after the startup raised $1 million from a16z as part of its “Speedrun” accelerator program, “a fast‐paced, 12-week startup program that guides founders through every critical stage of their growth.” Doublespeed markets its use of phone farms as a way to evade social media platforms’ policies against removing inauthentic behavior. Doublespeed customers get access to a dashboard that allows them to operate multiple AI-generated influencers. At the moment Doublespeed focuses on operating TikTok accounts, but also plans to give customers the ability to operate accounts on X and Instagram. Doublespeed was previously hacked in December of 2025. The data from that hack revealed at least 400 TikTok accounts Doublespeed operates and that at least 200 of those were actively promoting products on TikTok, mostly without disclosing that they are ads or not real people. Some of the products promoted by these AI-generated accounts included supplements, massagers, and dating apps. As we’ve noted last year, Marc Andreessen, after whom half of Andreessen Horowitz is named, also sits on Meta’s board of directors. Meta did not respond to our question about one of its board members backing a company that blatantly aims to violate its policy on “authentic identity representation.”
  3. About the author Joseph is an award-winning investigative journalist focused on generating impact. His work has triggered hundreds of millions of dollars worth of fines, shut down tech companies, and much more. About the author Emanuel Maiberg is interested in little known communities and processes that shape technology, troublemakers, and petty beefs. Email him at emanuel@404media.co

评论（默认中文）

n_u 2026/04/14 12:36
> 一家由 a16z 资助的初创公司，利用手机农场通过人工智能生成的 TikTok 账户充斥社交媒体

这不是机器人农场吗？它们不是已经存在了吗，并且不违反 TikTok 的服务条款吗？这篇文章最令人惊讶的是a16z投资了这个。

他们的网站也令人不安 https://doublespeed.ai/

> an a16z-funded startup that uses a phone farm to flood social media with AI-generated TikTok accounts

Isn't this a bot farm? Don't they already exist and aren't they against TikTok's terms of service? The most surprising part of this article is that a16z invested in this.

also their website is unsettling https://doublespeed.ai/

在 HN 中查看
OtherShrezzing 2026/04/14 13:31
A16z 的所有者对他们最近的社会不良态度持相当开放的态度。也许他们一直都是这样，只是过去比较私密。

无论如何，他们对这家公司的投资正好符合高资本反社会行为的趋势线。

A16z’s owners have been fairly open about their societally unpalatable attitudes of late. Maybe they were always like that, just a bit more private about it in the past.

In any case, their investment into this company just fits onto a trendline of high-capital antisocial behaviour.

在 HN 中查看
heavyset_go 2026/04/14 13:11
如果这让你感到惊讶，想象一下，当人们脑海中闪过“如果我雇佣一些黑客会怎样”的想法时，那些拥有太多资金的人会投资什么见不得人的狗屎。
If that surprises you, imagine the shady shit people with too much money invest in when the thought of "what if I hired some hackers" crosses their minds.
在 HN 中查看
ryandrake 2026/04/14 12:40
> 这篇文章最令人惊讶的是a16z投资了这个。

为什么这会让你感到惊讶？

> The most surprising part of this article is that a16z invested in this.

Why would this surprise you?

在 HN 中查看
ra 2026/04/14 13:05
让我惊讶的是。

我认为A16Z是一家想要创造长期价值的顶级VC。我没有在他们的重点领域看到黑帽社交媒体机器人农场。

这可能有点搞笑，但如果我向他们投资 1000 万美元，我就会询问他们的投资论文。

Surprises me to.

I thought A16Z were a top-tier VC wanting to create long-term value. I didn't see black-hat social media bot farms in their focus areas.

It might be a bit facetious, but if I had 10m invested with them I'd be asking questions about their investment thesis.

在 HN 中查看
Zanfa 2026/04/14 13:32
> 我认为A16Z是一家想要创造长期价值的顶级VC。

自从他们公开全力以赴进行加密货币诈骗以来，情况并非如此。

> 这可能有点搞笑，但如果我向他们投资 1000 万美元，我就会询问他们的投资论文。

此后，他们的基金规模猛增。

> I thought A16Z were a top-tier VC wanting to create long-term value.

That hasn’t been the case since they publicly went all-in into crypto scams.

> It might be a bit facetious, but if I had 10m invested with them I'd be asking questions about their investment thesis.

Their fund sizes have skyrocketed since.

mayneack 2026/04/14 13:34

A16Z was (is?) up to their eyeballs in crypto as well.
在 HN 中查看
sofixa 2026/04/14 13:07
是的，我们正在谈论风险投资。其中一个由两个人管理并以他们的名字命名，他们公开支持种族主义+厌女症+仇外心理+裙带关系，要求建造更多住房，同时阻止在自己的城市建造住房，等等。

他们投资巨魔农场是非常有名气的。

Yeah, we're talking a VC. And one run by and named after two guys who between them have publicly backed racism+misogyny+xenophobia+nepotism, have asked for more housing to be built while blocking housing in their own city, etc.

Them investing in a troll farm is pretty on brand.

在 HN 中查看
blargey 2026/04/14 12:31
看过之后 https://doublespeed.ai/ 出于病态的好奇心，我不得不说，简单的屏幕截图会更有效地发送消息。好吧，还有标语“a16z 资助了这个”。
Having looked at https://doublespeed.ai/ out of morbid curiosity, I have to say a simple screenshot would have sent the message more effectively. Well, that and the tagline "a16z funded this".

speedping 2026/04/14 13:18
我不是唯一一个认为这是一个基于运行 Apple A16Z SoC 的 iPhone 的手机农场的人（顺便说一句，它不存在，它是 A12Z 或 A16）
I can't be the only one who thought this was a phone farm based on iPhones running Apple A16Z SoC (which doesn't exist btw, it's A12Z or A16)

jim33442 2026/04/14 13:26
我们至少有两个人
There are at least two of us
在 HN 中查看
x3n0ph3n3 2026/04/14 12:25
任何在这样的公司工作的人都应该为自己深感羞愧。
Anyone working for a company like that should be deeply ashamed of themselves.
在 HN 中查看
liquidise 2026/04/14 13:25
对于我认为我们达成一致的原因，我有相反的反应。

多年来，社交媒体一直是一场透明的逐底竞争。越早变得无法修复越好。人工智能向他们提供大量内容应该有助于加速其下降，或者也许我给了普通用户（儿童以上）太多的信任。

I have an opposite reaction for what I assume are reasons we agree on.

Social media has been a transparent race to the bottom for many years. The sooner it is shittified beyond repair the better. AI flooding content to them should help speedrun its descent, or maybe I’ve giving the average user (above child age) too much credit.

在 HN 中查看
jim33442 2026/04/14 13:36
我想是的。这些网站可以通过依靠关注他们认识的人来加固，但这种溢出会破坏可发现性。这也是人们从旧的、更容易被抛弃的平台转向 TikTok 的部分原因。
I think so. These sites can be hardened by relying on people following who they know, but the slop ruins discoverability. That's also partially the reason people moved to TikTok from older, more dumped-on platforms.
